title: Office of Space Commerce to move ahead on mission authorization
summary:
The Commerce Department is moving ahead with plans to implement a voluntary mission authorization system for novel space activities that are not regulated by other agencies.
The post Office of Space Commerce to move ahead on mission authorization appeared first on SpaceNews.

title: Hubble Spies One-Sided Spiral
summary: This Hubble image features spiral galaxy NGC 4654, located 72 million light-years away in the constellation Virgo (the Maiden).

title: Why total solar eclipses are so special
summary: More than 2500 years ago, a total solar eclipse is said to have ended a war between the Lydians and the Medes. Today, we understand the science behind eclipses, but witnessing one is still one of the most awe-inspiring experiences on Earth.

title: Air Force takes over troubled military GPS receiver program
summary:
The Air Force Life Cycle Management Center will oversee development, integration and sustainment of equipment used to access the military’s encrypted GPS signal
The post Air Force takes over troubled military GPS receiver program appeared first on SpaceNews.

title: Two new Galileo satellites enter service
summary: Two new satellites have now officially joined Europe’s Galileo satellite navigation constellation, strengthening its capabilities, resilience and autonomy. Four Galileo First Generation satellites remain to be launched, with the next pair planned for the end of the year.

title: A Million-Panel Project
summary: Utah’s new solar power and battery storage facility is among the largest in the region.
